version: NIST AI RMF 1.0 / EU AI Act (Regulation 2024/1689)
source: Evidentia-authored (v0.9.3 P2.2) — initial concordance between NIST AI RMF subcategories and EU AI Act articles. Per-mapping confidence field surfaces low-confidence entries for SME review. Refinements via community PR.
v0_9_3_note: Initial v0.9.3 ship: ~35 high-confidence mappings covering Govern/Map/Measure/Manage to Articles 9, 10, 13, 14, 15, 16, 26, 27, 50, 72. Coverage of Articles 11, 12, 17, 18, 19, 51, 53, 55, 73 deferred to v0.9.4 SME pass.

confidence_rubric.high: Direct semantic match: AI RMF subcategory restates an EU AI Act article obligation.
confidence_rubric.medium: Overlapping intent with partial scope: AI RMF subcategory covers a subset or superset of the EU AI Act article.
confidence_rubric.low: Related domain but ambiguous: SME review recommended before relying on this mapping for compliance evidence.

mappings.source_control_title: Legal and regulatory requirements involving AI are understood
mappings.notes: Providers' obligation to understand + comply with EU AI Act regulatory requirements.

mappings.source_control_title: Characteristics of trustworthy AI are integrated into policies
mappings.notes: QMS in place ensuring compliance with trustworthy-AI characteristics.

mappings.source_control_title: Risk management activity levels determined
mappings.notes: Risk management system across the AI system lifecycle.

mappings.source_control_title: Roles, responsibilities, lines of communication documented
mappings.notes: Deployers' use in accordance with provider instructions implies role + responsibility definition.

mappings.source_control_title: Decision-making processes consider AI risk tolerances
mappings.notes: FRIA includes risk tolerance assessment for fundamental rights impact.

mappings.source_control_title: Risk-management culture is cultivated
mappings.notes: QMS implies organizational culture around quality + risk.

mappings.source_control_title: External stakeholder engagement
mappings.notes: Transparency to deployers overlaps with stakeholder engagement; SME review recommended.

mappings.source_control_title: Third-party engagement
mappings.notes: Providers manage third-party components (data, models) per Article 16 obligations.

mappings.source_control_title: Context is established and understood
mappings.notes: Classification of AI system requires understanding context (Annex III categorization).

mappings.source_control_title: Interdisciplinary AI team established
mappings.notes: Human oversight implies multi-disciplinary review capability; SME review recommended.

mappings.source_control_title: Tasks and methods are described
mappings.notes: Technical documentation includes task definition + methods.

mappings.source_control_title: Information about AI system knowledge limits and assumptions
mappings.notes: Transparency obligations include conveying knowledge limits + assumptions to deployers.

mappings.source_control_title: Scientific integrity and TEVV considerations
mappings.notes: Accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity == TEVV (test/evaluation/verification/validation).

mappings.source_control_title: Potential benefits and costs identified
mappings.notes: FRIA includes benefit/cost analysis from fundamental rights perspective.

mappings.source_control_title: Mapping risks based on impact
mappings.notes: Risk management system identifies + analyzes + evaluates risks.

mappings.source_control_title: Likelihood and impact of AI risks assessed
mappings.notes: Risk evaluation within the lifecycle risk management system.

mappings.source_control_title: Approaches and metrics for risk measurement
mappings.notes: Accuracy + robustness obligations imply measurement metrics.

mappings.source_control_title: Test sets, metrics, details of evaluation
mappings.notes: Data governance covers training/validation/testing dataset quality criteria.

mappings.source_control_title: Evaluations involve experts and end users
mappings.notes: Human oversight implies expert evaluation; end-user involvement is implicit.

mappings.source_control_title: AI system performance is regularly evaluated
mappings.notes: Post-market monitoring covers ongoing performance evaluation.

mappings.source_control_title: Approaches and metrics for trustworthy characteristics
mappings.notes: Trustworthy characteristics include accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity.

mappings.source_control_title: Measurement approaches for tracking applied
mappings.notes: Automatic log generation enables measurement tracking.

mappings.source_control_title: Risk responses considered with benefits
mappings.notes: Risk treatment is part of the Article 9 risk management system.

mappings.source_control_title: Resources allocated for risk management
mappings.notes: QMS implies resource allocation for compliance activities.

mappings.source_control_title: Risks and benefits documented and monitored
mappings.notes: Post-market monitoring documents ongoing risk + benefit posture.

mappings.source_control_title: Plans for incidents and continuous improvement
mappings.notes: Serious incident reporting is a specific incident plan obligation.
